scSidekick - Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA_pseudobulk

09_pathway_pseudobulk.Rmd

Overview

RunGSEA_pseudobulk() performs pseudobulk gene set enrichment analysis using sample-level limma-voom contrasts - a statistically rigorous alternative to the cell-level Wilcoxon approach in RunGSEA().

The key idea: cells from the same biological sample are aggregated into a pseudobulk profile before DE testing, so each biological replicate contributes one observation rather than thousands. This avoids pseudoreplication and produces calibrated p-values.

When to use each approach:

RunGSEA RunGSEA_pseudobulk
Input Any Seurat object Seurat object with sample metadata
DE method Wilcoxon (cell-level) limma-voom (sample-level)
Replicates needed No Yes (>=3 per group)
Controls confounders No Yes (covariates)
Best for Exploratory; no replicates Multi-sample studies

In addition to GSEA, RunGSEA_pseudobulk() optionally runs per-patient ssGSEA (set run.ssgsea = TRUE, the default) to produce individual-level pathway heatmaps showing patient-by-patient variability.

Standard setup 

library(scSidekick)
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)

# Assumes a multi-sample Seurat object with:
#   celltype  - annotated cell types
#   sample    - unique biological sample ID (one per donor/timepoint/condition)
#   condition - the comparison variable (e.g. "Control" vs "Disease")

1. Minimal call

The three required metadata columns are group.by (cell type), sample.by (sample/donor ID), and contrast.by (the condition to compare).

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

This runs GSEA for each cell type in group.by, comparing all levels of condition against each other with default limma-voom contrasts.

2. Specifying contrasts

By default, RunGSEA_pseudobulk tests all pairwise comparisons within contrast.by. Supply contrasts explicitly to control which comparisons are made, in which direction.

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  contrasts     = list(
    Disease_vs_Control = "Disease - Control",
    Treatment_vs_Control = "Treatment - Control"
  ),
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

Each name becomes the label for a set of output figures; each value is a standard limma contrast expression.

3. Adding covariates

Correct for batch effects or other sample-level metadata by passing them to covariates. These are included as additional terms in the limma-voom model.

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  covariates    = c("batch", "sex"),
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

4. Splitting by broad cell type

Use split.by to run the full pipeline independently within each major lineage. This is the pseudobulk equivalent of the split.by parameter in RunGSEA.

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype_fine",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  split.by      = "celltype_broad",
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

5. Custom pathway sets

Same syntax as RunGSEA. Default: Hallmark, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways.

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  pathway_sets  = list(
    Hallmark    = list(category = "H"),
    ImmuneSigDB = list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB")
  ),
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

6. Using a precomputed pseudobulk

If you have already aggregated pseudobulk counts (e.g. from ComputePseudobulk), pass them directly via pseudobulk to skip re-aggregation.

pb <- ComputePseudobulk(seu,
  group.by  = "celltype",
  sample.by = "sample")

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  pseudobulk = pb,
  group.by   = "celltype",
  sample.by  = "sample",
  contrast.by = "condition",
  output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

7. Per-patient ssGSEA heatmaps

run.ssgsea = TRUE (default) computes per-patient pathway activity via ssGSEA after the main GSEA, producing heatmaps where columns are individual samples. This makes patient-level variability visible alongside the group-level result.

Use ssgsea.use.padj = TRUE (default) to filter ssGSEA rows by BH-adjusted p-value, or ssgsea.use.padj = FALSE to use raw p-values.

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object  = seu,
  group.by       = "celltype",
  sample.by      = "sample",
  contrast.by    = "condition",
  run.ssgsea     = TRUE,
  ssgsea.use.padj = TRUE,
  top_n_ssgsea   = 20L,
  output_dir     = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

8. Customizing heatmap colors

The same heatmap_colors parameter works here as in RunGSEA. Pass any circlize::colorRamp2 object to override the default 5-stop blue-white-orange-red palette.

col_custom <- circlize::colorRamp2(
  c(-3, -1.5, 0, 1.5, 3),
  c("#762a83", "#c2a5cf", "white", "#7fbf7b", "#1b7837")
)

res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object  = seu,
  group.by       = "celltype",
  sample.by      = "sample",
  contrast.by    = "condition",
  heatmap_colors = col_custom,
  output_dir     = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

9. Resuming an interrupted run

save.rds = TRUE (default) saves intermediate RDS files after each cell type. If the run is interrupted, use redo_plots = TRUE to reload the saved RDS files and regenerate all PDFs without re-running the DE analysis.

# Regenerate all PDFs from saved RDS (no re-computation)
res <- RunGSEA_pseudobulk(
  seurat_object = seu,
  group.by      = "celltype",
  sample.by     = "sample",
  contrast.by   = "condition",
  redo_plots    = TRUE,
  output_dir    = "./Figures/Pathways_PB"
)

10. Return value

RunGSEA_pseudobulk returns a nested list, one element per cell type. Each element contains:

# GSEA results table for one cell type
res$`CD4 T`$gsea_results

# NES heatmap ggplot object
res$`CD4 T`$nes_heatmap

# ssGSEA score matrix (pathways x samples)
res$`CD4 T`$ssgsea_scores

Parameter reference

Parameter Type Default Description
seurat_object Seurat or NULL NULL Seurat object (use with pseudobulk to skip re-aggregation)
group.by character NULL Cell-type column; one GSEA run per level
sample.by character NULL Sample/donor ID column for pseudobulk aggregation
contrast.by character NULL Condition column for limma contrasts
split.by character or NULL NULL Run pipeline independently per level
subset.by character or NULL NULL Run pipeline on each subset separately
covariates character vector NULL Sample-level covariates added to limma model
contrasts named list NULL Named limma contrast expressions; NULL = all pairwise
pathway_sets named list Hallmark + KEGG + Reactome + WP MSigDB collections to test
species character "human" Species for msigdbr
assay character or NULL NULL Seurat assay containing raw counts
min.cells integer 10 Minimum cells a sample must contribute per cell type
min.samples.per.group integer 3 Minimum samples per condition level
run.ssgsea logical TRUE Also run per-patient ssGSEA
ssgsea.use.padj logical TRUE Use BH-adjusted p-value for ssGSEA row selection
nes.cutoff numeric 1.0 Minimum absolute NES for heatmap inclusion
top_n_heatmap integer 30 Max pathways shown in NES heatmaps
top_n_ssgsea integer 30 Max pathways shown in ssGSEA heatmaps
heatmap_colors colorRamp2 or NULL NULL Custom heatmap color function
heatmap_params named list list(...) Extra args forwarded to ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap()
save.rds logical TRUE Save intermediate RDS after each cell type
redo_plots logical FALSE Reload saved RDS and regenerate PDFs only
caffeinate logical FALSE Prevent system sleep during the run

See also

  • Vignette 08: RunGSEA - cell-level Wilcoxon-based GSEA (no replicates needed)
  • Vignette 10: RunSCssGSEA - per-cell pathway scoring; PlotPathwayButterfly - 4-pathway state hierarchy
  • Vignette 11: RunCellChat - pathway-level cell-cell communication

Session info 

sessionInfo()
